methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Antares
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Chris Hegna, currently valued at $200M+ post-money following $100M+ in total capital raised. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 comparable companies (3 public, 8 private), including peers such as Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear subsector, peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable. The Stage-Progression method proved usable, applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B to subsequent funding rounds. This method, anchored to Type One's $200M last post-money valuation, yields an implied valuation of $380M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $323M to $437M, with a median of $380M. This relatively narrow range reflects reliance on a single triangulation method rather than corroboration across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the comparability of nuclear energy peers across distinct technology pathways (advanced reactors, microreactors, fusion enablement), limited public market data points, and the stage-progression multiplier's applicability to early-stage frontier tech. Additional precision would require either a broader peer set or direct market transaction data within the nuclear subsector.
